content.thinking:
Two issues:
1. The StatefulSet has only 1 replica but there should be 3 nodes. I need to scale it back to 3.
2. The producer gets "Access refused" on `/app` vhost - I need to check and fix permissions.

Also, rabbitmq-2 is Terminating and only 2 nodes (rabbitmq-0 and rabbitmq-1) are running. The StatefulSet replica count is 1, which explains why rabbitmq-2 is being deleted. I need to scale it back to 3.

Let me check the producer deployment to understand what user credentials it's using.

content.text: Two issues found: StatefulSet is scaled down to 1 (should be 3), and the producer has access refused on `/app` vhost. Let me get more details.

content.thinking:
`app-user` has permissions on `/app` but the configure permission is empty (not `.*`). That's the bug! The app-user needs configure permissions to declare queues. Let me fix the permissions.

- configure: (empty) - this means it can't configure (declare/delete queues, exchanges, etc.)
- write: .* - can publish
- read: .* - can consume

The producer fails with "Access refused" when trying to declare the queue. Queue declaration requires the configure permission. I need to set configure to `.*` for app-user in `/app`.

content.text: Found the bug. `app-user` has no configure permission on `/app` vhost — the configure field is empty, so it can't declare queues. Let me fix that.

input.command: kubectl -n karma-rabbitmq-skip-upgrade-3-9-to-4-8747b50d-cluster-a exec rabbitmq-0 -- rabbitmqctl set_permissions -p /app app-user '.*' '.*' '.*' 2>&1
input.description: Grant app-user full configure/write/read on /app vhost
